text: A 67-year-old man presents to the emergency department with chest pain that started 2 hours ago. The pain is described as crushing, radiates to his left arm, and is associated with diaphoresis and nausea. His blood pressure is 150/90 mmHg, pulse is 110/min, and an ECG shows ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. Which of the following is the most appropriate immediate treatment?
ground_truth: Percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) or thrombolytic therapy

text: A 45-year-old woman presents with fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance that has progressively worsened over the past 6 months. Physical examination reveals dry skin, bradycardia, and delayed relaxation of deep tendon reflexes. Laboratory tests show TSH of 15 mIU/L (normal: 0.4-4.0) and free T4 of 0.6 ng/dL (normal: 0.9-1.7). What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old man presents with a 3-week history of fever, night sweats, and productive cough with blood-tinged sputum. He recently immigrated from a country with high tuberculosis prevalence. Chest X-ray shows upper lobe cavitary lesions. What is the most appropriate initial diagnostic test?
ground_truth: Sputum acid-fast bacilli (AFB) smear and culture

text: A 72-year-old woman is brought to the emergency department by her daughter who noticed sudden onset of right-sided weakness and slurred speech 45 minutes ago. The patient has a history of atrial fibrillation but is not on anticoagulation. Which imaging modality should be performed first?

A 55-year-old man with a history of chronic alcohol use presents with hematemesis. On examination, he has spider angiomas, palmar erythema, and ascites. His platelet count is 60,000/μL. What is the most likely cause of his bleeding?

A 6-month-old infant is brought to the clinic for routine vaccination. The mother mentions the baby has been irritable and has had several episodes of projectile vomiting after feeds for the past 2 weeks. On examination, a small, firm, olive-shaped mass is palpated in the right upper quadrant. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 32-year-old woman at 34 weeks gestation presents with severe headache and visual disturbances. Her blood pressure is 170/110 mmHg, and urinalysis shows 3+ proteinuria. Which medication should be given immediately to prevent seizures?

A 25-year-old medical student presents with a 6-month history of excessive worry about multiple aspects of life, difficulty concentrating, muscle tension, and insomnia. These symptoms significantly interfere with academic performance. What is the most appropriate first-line pharmacological treatment?

A 35-year-old woman presents with symmetric joint pain and morning stiffness lasting more than 1 hour in her hands and wrists for the past 3 months. Laboratory tests show positive anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide (anti-CCP) antibodies and elevated ESR. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 68-year-old man with a 40 pack-year smoking history presents with progressive dyspnea and chronic cough. Spirometry shows FEV1/FVC ratio of 0.65 and FEV1 of 45% predicted. Chest X-ray shows hyperinflation. What is the most appropriate long-term management?

A 45-year-old man with type 2 diabetes presents for routine follow-up. His blood pressure is 145/92 mmHg, and urinalysis shows microalbuminuria. His current medications include metformin. Which class of medication should be added to protect kidney function?

A 65-year-old woman presents with fatigue and easy bruising. Laboratory tests reveal hemoglobin 8.5 g/dL, platelet count 40,000/μL, and WBC 85,000/μL with 80% blasts. Peripheral smear shows Auer rods. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old man presents with a rapidly spreading, painful, erythematous rash on his leg following a minor cut. The affected area is warm, swollen, and has poorly defined borders. He has fever and chills. What is the most appropriate empiric antibiotic treatment?

A 22-year-old woman is brought to the ED after being found unconscious at home. She has pinpoint pupils, respiratory rate of 6/min, and track marks on her arms. What is the most appropriate immediate intervention?

A 56-year-old man presents with sudden onset severe abdominal pain and vomiting. On examination, he has abdominal distension, high-pitched bowel sounds, and a tender, irreducible mass in the right groin. What is the most likely diagnosis?
ground_truth: Incarcerated inguinal hernia with bowel obstruction

text: A 62-year-old woman with a 30 pack-year smoking history presents with a 3-month history of persistent cough and 10-pound weight loss. Chest CT shows a 4 cm spiculated mass in the right upper lobe with mediastinal lymphadenopathy. What is the next best step in management?

A 58-year-old man with diabetes and hypertension presents with exertional chest pain that is relieved by rest. Stress test shows reversible ischemia in the LAD territory. Coronary angiography reveals 80% stenosis of the proximal LAD. What is the most appropriate treatment?

A 24-year-old woman presents with dysuria, urinary frequency, and suprapubic pain for 2 days. Urinalysis shows pyuria and bacteriuria. She has no fever or flank pain. What is the most appropriate empiric treatment?

A 28-year-old woman presents with episodes of unilateral throbbing headache associated with nausea, photophobia, and phonophobia. The headaches last 4-12 hours and are preceded by visual auras. Neurological examination is normal between attacks. What is the most appropriate preventive treatment?
A 38-year-old woman presents with amenorrhea, galactorrhea, and headaches. MRI shows a 12 mm pituitary adenoma. Laboratory tests reveal elevated prolactin levels. What is the first-line treatment?

A 45-year-old man presents with chronic diarrhea, abdominal pain, and weight loss. Colonoscopy shows skip lesions with cobblestone appearance and deep ulcers. Biopsy reveals non-caseating granulomas. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 3-year-old boy is brought to the ED with barking cough, inspiratory stridor, and mild respiratory distress that started 2 days ago with URI symptoms. His temperature is 38.2°C. What is the most appropriate initial treatment?

A 19-year-old college student is brought by friends after exhibiting bizarre behavior, auditory hallucinations, and paranoid delusions for the past week. He has no previous psychiatric history. Urine drug screen is negative. What is the most appropriate initial treatment?

A 50-year-old woman presents with proximal muscle weakness, difficulty climbing stairs, and elevated CPK levels. Physical exam shows heliotrope rash and Gottron's papules. What additional test should be ordered to screen for associated conditions?

A 35-year-old man presents with sudden onset pleuritic chest pain and dyspnea. He is tall and thin with no significant medical history. Chest X-ray shows absence of lung markings in the upper right hemithorax with a visible pleural line. What is the most appropriate management?
ground_truth: Needle decompression or chest tube placement

text: A 12-year-old boy presents with cola-colored urine, facial puffiness, and hypertension 2 weeks after recovering from strep throat. Urinalysis shows RBC casts and proteinuria. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 28-year-old African American woman presents with fatigue and jaundice. Labs show hemoglobin 7.5 g/dL, reticulocyte count 8%, and indirect bilirubin 4.2 mg/dL. Peripheral smear shows sickle cells. She reports severe pain in her legs and back. What is the most appropriate immediate treatment?

A 45-year-old man presents with silvery scales on erythematous plaques over his elbows and knees. The lesions have been present for years and are mildly pruritic. He also complains of joint pain in his fingers. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 55-year-old man presents with severe, tearing chest pain that radiates to his back. He has a history of hypertension. Blood pressure is 180/100 mmHg in the right arm and 140/80 mmHg in the left arm. Chest X-ray shows widened mediastinum. What is the most appropriate next diagnostic test?

A 28-year-old woman at 38 weeks gestation presents with regular painful contractions every 3 minutes. Cervical examination reveals 5 cm dilation, 80% effacement, and intact membranes. Fetal heart rate tracing is reassuring. What is the appropriate management?
ground_truth: Admit for active labor and continue monitoring

text: A 72-year-old man presents with painless jaundice, weight loss, and a palpable gallbladder. CT scan shows a mass in the head of the pancreas with dilated intrahepatic ducts. CA 19-9 is elevated. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 65-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea and lower extremity edema. Echocardiography shows left ventricular ejection fraction of 30%, dilated left ventricle, and mitral regurgitation. She has no history of myocardial infarction. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 40-year-old woman presents with palpitations, weight loss despite increased appetite, and tremor. Physical exam reveals exophthalmos and diffuse thyroid enlargement. TSH is <0.01 mIU/L and free T4 is elevated. What is the most specific diagnostic test?

A newborn boy is noted to have cyanosis shortly after birth that worsens with crying. Physical examination reveals a single S2 and a harsh systolic murmur. Chest X-ray shows a 'boot-shaped' heart. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 60-year-old man presents with resting tremor, bradykinesia, and rigidity that started on his right side 6 months ago. He also reports constipation and loss of smell. What is the most appropriate initial treatment?

A 32-year-old man with HIV (CD4 count 150 cells/μL) presents with fever, productive cough, and dyspnea. Chest X-ray shows bilateral interstitial infiltrates. Induced sputum is positive for Pneumocystis jirovecii. What is the treatment of choice?

A 52-year-old man with chronic hepatitis C presents with confusion and asterixis. Ammonia level is 120 μmol/L (normal: 15-45). He has no signs of GI bleeding. What is the first-line treatment for his condition?
A 70-year-old woman presents with severe headache, jaw claudication, and visual disturbances. ESR is 110 mm/hr and CRP is markedly elevated. What is the most appropriate immediate treatment?

A 35-year-old woman presents with recurrent episodes of palpitations, sweating, trembling, and fear of dying that last about 10 minutes. Between episodes, she constantly worries about having another attack. What is the most likely diagnosis?
A 25-year-old woman with heavy menstrual bleeding presents with fatigue and pallor. Labs show hemoglobin 7.8 g/dL, MCV 68 fL, and ferritin 8 ng/mL. What is the most appropriate treatment?

A 55-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea and dry cough for 6 months. She has a history of rheumatoid arthritis. HRCT shows honeycombing and traction bronchiectasis. Pulmonary function tests show restrictive pattern. What is the most likely diagnosis?
A 55-year-old man with diabetes presents with frothy urine and lower extremity edema. Labs show albumin 2.5 g/dL, proteinuria 4.5 g/24hr, and cholesterol 280 mg/dL. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 45-year-old construction worker presents after falling from a ladder. He complains of back pain and inability to move his legs. Examination reveals loss of sensation below the umbilicus and absent lower extremity reflexes. What is the most appropriate initial imaging?

A 48-year-old woman presents with a 2 cm breast lump. Mammography shows a spiculated mass with microcalcifications. Core needle biopsy confirms invasive ductal carcinoma, ER/PR positive, HER2 negative. What is the next step in management?
ground_truth: Surgical excision (lumpectomy or mastectomy) with sentinel lymph node biopsy

text: A 7-year-old boy presents with honey-crusted lesions around his mouth and nose that started as vesicles 3 days ago. The lesions are pruritic but not painful. He has been afebrile. What is the most appropriate treatment?
ground_truth: Topical mupirocin or oral antibiotics (for extensive disease)

text: A 35-year-old woman at 20 weeks gestation has an abnormal quad screen suggesting increased risk for Down syndrome. What is the most appropriate next step?
ground_truth: Offer genetic counseling and diagnostic testing (amniocentesis or cell-free DNA testing)

text: A 60-year-old man presents with acute onset severe left lower quadrant pain, fever, and leukocytosis. CT scan shows sigmoid colon wall thickening with pericolic fat stranding. What is the most appropriate initial management?
ground_truth: Antibiotics, bowel rest, and IV fluids

text: A 15-month-old child is brought for well-child visit. Parents report the child is not yet walking and has only 3 words. Physical exam shows macrocephaly and hypotonia. What is the most appropriate next step?
ground_truth: Developmental assessment and possible referral for early intervention

text: A 70-year-old man presents with syncope during exertion. Physical examination reveals a crescendo-decrescendo systolic murmur that radiates to the carotids. Echocardiography shows severe aortic stenosis with mean gradient of 50 mmHg. What is the definitive treatment?

A 30-year-old woman presents with irregular periods, hirsutism, and obesity. She has been trying to conceive for 2 years without success. Laboratory tests show elevated testosterone and LH/FSH ratio of 3:1. What is the most likely diagnosis?
